title: Question: which is faster for selecting many rows by label — loc or reindex?
body: I have a DataFrame with ~10M rows indexed by a unique ID column and a list of ~50k IDs I want to fetch. Is `df.loc[id_list]` faster than `df.reindex(id_list)`? Are they equivalent? The docs don't compare them.

comments.body: `loc` is for selection — it raises on missing labels (or silently in older versions). `reindex` is for alignment — it returns NaN for missing labels. If your `id_list` is known to be a subset of the index, they're equivalent in speed for a single call. If it isn't, `reindex` is what you want.
